name: Lemon Meringue Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup egg-white or fat-free substitute (it works well)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drape one sheet of dough across a 9 or 10 inch pie plate. Press into the plate, folding overhanging edges toward the center and crumpling to fit. Coat the top with a misting of no-stick spray, then repeat with the remaining dough sheets. Bake at 375°F for 5-7 minutes, or until golden brown. Let cool on a rack.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, whisk together water and cornstarch until starch is dissolved. Whisk in honey and lemon juice. Place over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil and runs clear. Remove from heat and slowly whisk in egg substitute. Return to heat and cook, stirring, until thickened. Add grated lemon rind. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
3. In a large bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Add cream of tartar and continue beating until soft peaks form. Drizzle in honey and beat until stiff but not dry.
4. Spread meringue over the pie so it covers the filling and touches the crust on all sides. Make small swirls with the back of a spoon. Broil about 6 inches from the heat until lightly browned, being careful not to burn it. Serve at room temperature.
